title: TickTally
description: Track your true Minecraft playtime across worlds and servers with clean, automatic tracking and zero fluff. TickTally keeps it simple, accurate, and entirely under your control
body:
# TickTally

A clean, no-nonsense playtime tracker for Minecraft.

TickTally tracks your **actual in-game time** across **singleplayer and multiplayer**, combining everything into one simple total — no guesswork, no menus, no nonsense.

## Give your feedback/experience at:
- FlossyFish63943 [Discord]
OR
- aaronabrm@gmail.com [Email]

---

## Features

### Smart Total Playtime
- Tracks **real time spent playing** (not just time the game is open)
- Combines **singleplayer + multiplayer** into one total
- Updates automatically while you play



### Singleplayer Bulk Import
- On first run, TickTally scans all your worlds
- Imports existing playtime from Minecraft stats
- Never double-counts worlds
- Safely ignores deleted worlds (no data loss)



### Multiplayer Support
- Tracks time on servers in real time
- Works across lobbies, sub-servers, and reconnects
- Manual adjustment supported (because reality isn’t perfect)


### Commands

- View your total playtime
```
/ticktally view
```

- Manaully add time (multiplayer only)
```
/ticktally add <minutes>
```

- Manually subtract time (multiplayer only)
```
/ticktally remove <minutes>
```

- Help with commands
```
/ticktally help
```

### UI Integration
- Shows total playtime on the **title screen**
- Displays a subtle message in the **multiplayer menu**
- Clean, minimal, and always out of your way
- 
### Mod Menu Support
- Configure settings easily through Mod Menu
- Toggle UI elements
- Adjust behavior without touching files

### Built to Be Safe
- JSON storage with automatic recovery
- Handles corrupted or missing data gracefully
- Never deletes your tracked time

## Philosophy

TickTally is **not a parenting tool**.

It doesn’t judge, limit, or control how you play.

It simply tells you the truth:
> how much time you’ve actually spent in the game.

What you do with that information is entirely up to you.

## How It Works

- Uses Minecraft’s built-in stats for initial world imports
- Tracks active playtime using client ticks
- Saves progress periodically to prevent loss
- Stores everything locally in:
```
.minecraft/config/ticktally.json
```
- Stores corrupted saves in:
```
.minecraft/config/corrupt_<total_played_milliseconds_at_the_time_of_corruption>_ticktally.json
```


## Installation

1. Install Fabric Loader
2. Install Fabric API
3. Drop the `.jar` into your `mods` folder
4. Launch the game
5. Also, you are **required** to have [Fabric API](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-api), [Cloth Config API](https://modrinth.com/mod/cloth-config) & [Mod Menu](https://modrinth.com/mod/modmenu) installed too.

Done.


## Why TickTally?

Minecraft doesn’t tell you how long you’ve *actually* played.

TickTally fills that gap:
- No bloated UI
- No unnecessary features
- No invasive tracking

Just a **single number that quietly grows with your journey**

## License

MIT License

## Final Note

This mod was built with a simple goal:
make something _small_, _useful_, and _honest_.

If it helps you even a _little_ — it did its job.

And I, the creator of this mod, **only** make _any_ software when I either want to learn something or felt the need of it and thought that others might need this too, and this project, goes into the second category.
